framework: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (NIST AI 100-1)
element_extraction_methodology: Each element represents one subcategory outcome statement from the NIST AI RMF 1.0 Core. Subcategories are the lowest-level actionable outcomes within the framework's GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE structure. Each subcategory describes a specific organizational or technical outcome that contributes to AI risk management. Element text preserves the original NIST outcome statement; competency_implication field provides the workforce competency interpretation used for scoring. This is a regulatory/standards STRM using dual-provenance methodology consistent with STRM-005-EU-AI-ACT (see ADR-019).
scoring_mode_note: All elements scored via Cross-Encoder STS as primary (consistent with STRM-001 through STRM-005). The competency_implication field bridges the vocabulary gap between risk management outcome language and competency language. Rationale type is Functional per NIST IR 8477 — mapping direction is outcome→competency (what workforce capability achieves this outcome?).

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI-related legal and regulatory requirements, including the ability to identify applicable regulations, assess organizational compliance status, manage regulatory obligations, and maintain documentation of legal requirements across the AI lifecycle.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies, procedures, and processes.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in integrating trustworthy AI principles — including validity, reliability, safety, security, resilience, accountability, transparency, explainability, interpretability, privacy, and fairness — into organizational governance documents, operational procedures, and business processes.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place to determine the needed level of risk management activities based on the organization's risk tolerance.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to design and implement risk management processes calibrated to organizational risk tolerance, including risk appetite definition, threshold-setting for AI systems, and escalation procedures that reflect the organization's acceptable risk levels.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: The risk management process and its outcomes are established through transparent policies, procedures, and other controls based on organizational risk priorities.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in establishing transparent AI risk management governance, including development of risk policies, procedural controls, and accountability mechanisms that reflect organizational risk priorities and enable stakeholder visibility into risk management outcomes.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk management process and its outcomes are planned, organizational roles and responsibilities are clearly defined, including determining the frequency of periodic review.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI risk management monitoring and review design, including defining organizational roles and responsibilities for risk oversight, establishing review frequency and methodology, and planning continuous monitoring activities for AI risk management effectiveness.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced according to organizational risk priorities.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing and maintaining AI system inventory mechanisms, including cataloging deployed AI systems, tracking system attributes relevant to risk assessment, and allocating inventory resources according to organizational risk priorities.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
elements.outcome_text: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or decrease the organization's trustworthiness.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system decommissioning procedures, including safe phase-out planning, risk assessment during decommissioning, data handling and retention requirements, stakeholder communication, and maintaining organizational trustworthiness throughout the system retirement process.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
elements.outcome_text: Roles and responsibilities and lines of communication related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks are documented and are clear to individuals and teams throughout the organization.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in defining and documenting AI risk management roles, responsibilities, and communication structures, including RACI-style accountability assignment, cross-functional coordination mechanisms, and ensuring clarity of risk management expectations across organizational levels.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
elements.outcome_text: The organization's personnel and partners receive AI risk management training to enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies, procedures, and agreements.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing and delivering AI risk management training programs for organizational personnel and external partners, including needs assessment, curriculum development, training delivery, competency evaluation, and alignment with organizational policies and contractual agreements.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
elements.outcome_text: Executive leadership of the organization takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of executive-level AI risk governance, including board and C-suite accountability structures, executive decision-making frameworks for AI risk acceptance, and leadership responsibility models for AI system development and deployment decisions.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
elements.outcome_text: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle is informed by a diverse team (e.g., demographic diversity, disciplinary expertise, domain experience, and formal and informal training).
elements.competency_implication: Skill in building and managing diverse AI risk management teams, including assembling cross-disciplinary expertise, incorporating varied domain experience, ensuring demographic representation, and integrating diverse perspectives into AI risk decision-making processes.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
elements.outcome_text: Policies and procedures are in place to define and differentiate roles and responsibilities for human-AI configurations and oversight of AI systems.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of human-AI interaction governance, including defining roles for human oversight of AI systems, establishing human-in-the-loop and human-on-the-loop configurations, and creating policies that differentiate responsibilities between human operators and AI system functions.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
elements.outcome_text: Organizational policies and practices are in place to foster a critical thinking and safety-first mindset in the design, development, deployment, and uses of AI systems to minimize potential negative impacts.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in cultivating organizational AI safety culture, including embedding critical thinking practices into AI development workflows, establishing safety-first design principles, and creating organizational norms that prioritize harm prevention in AI system design, development, deployment, and use.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
elements.outcome_text: Organizational teams document the risks and potential impacts of the AI technology they design, develop, deploy, or use, and communicate about the impacts more broadly.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI risk documentation and communication, including documenting identified risks and potential impacts of AI systems, tailoring risk communication for different stakeholder audiences, and establishing transparency practices for internal and external risk reporting.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
elements.outcome_text: Organizational practices are in place to enable AI testing, identification of incidents, and information sharing.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI testing and incident management infrastructure, including establishing testing environments and protocols, implementing AI incident identification and tracking mechanisms, and creating information-sharing practices for AI-related findings across organizational boundaries.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Organizational policies and practices are in place to collect, consider, prioritize, and integrate feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system regarding the potential individual and societal impacts related to AI risks.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in external stakeholder engagement for AI risk management, including designing feedback collection mechanisms, prioritizing external input on individual and societal AI impacts, and integrating stakeholder feedback into AI risk assessment and mitigation processes.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Mechanisms are established to enable AI actors to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and implementation.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing feedback incorporation mechanisms for AI systems, including establishing adjudication processes for conflicting feedback, creating systematic channels for AI actor input, and integrating adjudicated feedback into ongoing AI system design and implementation cycles.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
elements.outcome_text: Policies and procedures are in place that address AI risks associated with third-party entities, including risks of infringement of a third-party's intellectual property or other rights.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI supply chain risk management, including assessing third-party AI component risks, evaluating intellectual property implications of third-party AI software and data, and establishing contractual and procedural safeguards for third-party AI relationships.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
elements.outcome_text: Contingency processes are in place for third-party AI systems or data.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in developing contingency plans for third-party AI dependencies, including backup strategies for third-party system failures, data continuity planning, alternative sourcing procedures, and business continuity measures for AI supply chain disruptions.
elements.function_description: A culture of risk management is cultivated and present.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: Intended purposes, potentially beneficial uses, context of use, and requirements known to the team are understood and documented. Assumptions and limitations for each are identified.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system context documentation, including articulating intended purposes and beneficial uses, documenting operational context and known requirements, and identifying and recording assumptions and limitations relevant to AI system deployment.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: Interdisciplinary AI actors, competencies, skills, and capacities for establishing context reflect demographic diversity and broad domain and user experience expertise, and their roles are documented and understood.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of interdisciplinary team composition requirements for AI context-setting, including assembling diverse domain expertise, ensuring demographic representation, documenting team roles and competencies, and leveraging broad user experience to inform AI system context.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: The business value or context of business use has been clearly defined or—in the case of assessing existing AI systems—the ongoing relevance of the AI system to business requirements and its context of use has been evaluated.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to evaluate AI system business value and relevance, including defining business use cases for new AI systems, assessing ongoing business alignment of existing AI systems, and conducting relevance evaluations against evolving business requirements and operational contexts.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: The organization's mission and relevant goals for AI technology are understood.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of organizational mission alignment for AI technology, including understanding how AI systems relate to organizational strategic objectives, interpreting mission-relevant goals in terms of AI capabilities, and ensuring AI technology decisions align with organizational purpose.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: Organizational risk tolerances are determined and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in determining and documenting organizational AI risk tolerance, including conducting risk appetite assessments, defining quantitative and qualitative risk thresholds for AI systems, and establishing documented risk tolerance statements that guide AI development and deployment decisions.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Context is established and understood.
elements.outcome_text: System requirements (e.g., human-AI teaming configurations, testing, safety and performance goals) and design decisions are documented based on an AI system's intended purpose and context of use.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in documenting AI system requirements and design decisions, including specifying human-AI teaming configurations, defining testing protocols, establishing safety and performance goals, and tracing design decisions to intended purpose and operational context.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
elements.outcome_text: The specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks that the AI system will support are defined (e.g., classifiers, generative models, recommenders).
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system task specification and method selection, including defining the specific tasks an AI system will perform, selecting appropriate implementation methods (classification, generation, recommendation), and documenting the relationship between tasks and implementation approaches.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
elements.outcome_text: Information about the AI system's knowledge limits and how system output may be utilized and overseen by humans is documented. Documentation includes information about AI system assumptions and limitations relevant to each of the trustworthiness characteristics.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in documenting AI system knowledge limits, human oversight requirements, and trustworthiness assumptions, including specifying system output utilization boundaries, defining human oversight mechanisms, and recording limitations relevant to each trustworthy AI characteristic.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
elements.outcome_text: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented, including those related to experimental design, data collection and selection, data processing and data curation, bias, and the factors related to the assessment of the credibility and reliability of the AI technology being used or developed.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI scientific integrity and test, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) methodology, including experimental design for AI systems, data collection and curation practices, bias identification and documentation, and credibility and reliability assessment of AI technologies.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
elements.outcome_text: Potential benefits of intended functionality are examined and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to assess and document potential benefits of AI system functionality, including conducting benefit analysis, identifying value propositions for stakeholders, and documenting expected positive outcomes from AI system deployment.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
elements.outcome_text: Potential costs, including non-monetary costs, which result from expected or realized AI errors or system functionality and trustworthiness—as connected to organizational risk tolerance—are examined and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI cost-risk analysis, including evaluating monetary and non-monetary costs of AI errors, assessing trustworthiness failures against organizational risk tolerance, and documenting the relationship between system functionality, error rates, and organizational impact.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
elements.outcome_text: Targeted application scope is specified and documented based on the system's capability, established context, and AI system requirements.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in defining and documenting AI system application scope, including specifying deployment boundaries based on system capabilities, aligning scope with established operational context, and documenting scope constraints derived from AI system requirements.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
elements.outcome_text: Risks and benefits of all identified TEVV approaches are assessed and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to evaluate and compare TEVV approach trade-offs for AI systems, including assessing the risks and benefits of different testing, evaluation, verification, and validation methodologies, and documenting the rationale for selected TEVV approaches.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
elements.outcome_text: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, public incident reports, feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, or other data are identified and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI impact likelihood and magnitude assessment, including using historical data, public incident reports, external feedback, and comparable system analysis to identify, quantify, and document the likelihood and severity of potential AI system impacts.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.
elements.outcome_text: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components—including the use of third-party data or software—are in place, followed, and documented, as are risks of infringement of a third-party's intellectual property or other rights.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI component risk mapping methodology, including assessing legal and technology risks of AI system components, evaluating third-party data and software risks, identifying intellectual property infringement risks, and documenting component-level risk assessments.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.
elements.outcome_text: Internal risk controls for components of the AI system, including third-party AI technologies, are identified and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in identifying and documenting internal risk controls for AI system components, including evaluating control effectiveness for third-party AI technologies, mapping control coverage across system architecture, and documenting control mechanisms and their operational parameters.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized.
elements.outcome_text: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, public incident reports, feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, or other data are identified and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in societal and individual impact assessment for AI systems, including characterizing impacts across different population groups, analyzing historical AI deployment outcomes, incorporating public incident data and external stakeholder feedback, and documenting impact likelihood and magnitude for diverse affected communities.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized.
elements.outcome_text: Practices and personnel for supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, negative, and unanticipated impacts are in place and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI stakeholder engagement practices, including establishing regular engagement mechanisms with affected communities, designing feedback integration processes for unanticipated impacts, and documenting engagement personnel and practices for ongoing AI impact monitoring.
elements.function_description: Context is recognized and risks related to context are identified.

elements.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
elements.outcome_text: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function—including risks of third-party entities—are identified and documented, and the approaches include: rigorous software testing and performance evaluation approaches; assessment of AI systems for adherence to requirements and trustworthiness characteristics; assessment of whether AI system performance meets legal and regulatory requirements or industry standards; ongoing monitoring and reassessment of AI system performance against organizational risk tolerances.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing AI risk measurement approaches and metrics, including software testing and performance evaluation methodology, trustworthiness characteristic assessment, compliance measurement against legal, regulatory, and industry standards, and ongoing performance monitoring against organizational risk tolerances.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
elements.outcome_text: Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing measures are regularly assessed and updated, including reports of errors and impacts on affected communities.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to evaluate and update AI measurement effectiveness, including assessing metric appropriateness over time, incorporating error reports and community impact data into measurement refinement, and maintaining measurement system currency as AI systems and their impacts evolve.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
elements.outcome_text: Internal experts who did not serve as combatants of the system and/or independent assessors are involved in regular assessments and updates. Domain experts, users, AI actors external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, and affected communities are consulted in support of assessments as necessary per organizational risk tolerance.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of independent AI assessment practices, including engaging internal experts outside the development team, coordinating independent assessor reviews, consulting domain experts and affected communities, and structuring assessment involvement according to organizational risk tolerance levels.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Test sets, metrics, and details about the tools used during test, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI TEVV documentation, including recording test set compositions and selection criteria, documenting metrics and measurement tools, and maintaining comprehensive records of testing, evaluation, verification, and validation activities and their results.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements (including those related to informed consent) and are representative of the relevant population.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of human subjects evaluation requirements for AI systems, including informed consent procedures, population representativeness in evaluation design, ethical review board requirements, and ensuring evaluations meet applicable legal and ethical standards for human participation.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: AI system performance or assurance criteria are measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for conditions similar to deployment conditions. Measurements are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system performance measurement under deployment-representative conditions, including designing qualitative and quantitative performance assessments, establishing assurance criteria, conducting measurements in simulated deployment environments, and documenting measurement results and conditions.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: The functionality and behavior of the AI system and its components—as identified in the MAP function—are monitored when in production.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in production AI system monitoring, including implementing observability for AI system functionality and behavior, tracking component-level performance against MAP-phase specifications, and maintaining production monitoring aligned with pre-deployment risk assessments.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable. Limitations of the generalizability beyond the conditions under which the technology was developed are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system validity and reliability demonstration, including validation methodology, reliability testing, generalizability limitation identification, and documenting the boundaries of system applicability beyond development conditions.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: The AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks—as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be safe, its residual negative risk does not exceed the risk tolerance, and it can fail safely, particularly if made to operate beyond its knowledge limits. Safety metrics reflect system reliability and robustness, real-time monitoring, and response times for AI system failures.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI safety evaluation and safe failure design, including conducting regular safety risk assessments, demonstrating residual risk within tolerance, designing fail-safe mechanisms, establishing safety metrics for reliability, robustness, and response time, and implementing real-time safety monitoring.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: AI system security and resilience—as identified in the MAP function—are evaluated and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system security and resilience evaluation, including assessing AI-specific attack surfaces, evaluating system resilience to adversarial inputs and operational disruptions, and documenting security posture against risks identified during the MAP function.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Risks associated with transparency and accountability—as identified in the MAP function—are examined and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in evaluating AI transparency and accountability risks, including assessing the adequacy of explainability mechanisms, evaluating accountability structures for AI system decisions, and documenting transparency and accountability risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: The AI model is explained, validated, and documented, and AI system output is interpreted within its context—as identified in the MAP function—to inform responsible use and governance.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI model explanation, validation, and contextual interpretation, including developing model explanations appropriate for different audiences, validating model behavior and outputs, and interpreting AI system outputs within their operational context to support responsible use and governance decisions.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Privacy risk of the AI system—as identified in the MAP function—is examined and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI privacy risk assessment, including examining privacy risks throughout the AI lifecycle, evaluating data protection adequacy, assessing re-identification and inference risks, and documenting privacy risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Fairness and bias—as identified in the MAP function—are evaluated and the results are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI fairness and bias evaluation, including applying fairness metrics, detecting and measuring bias in AI system outputs, assessing disparate impact across population groups, and documenting evaluation results against MAP-phase fairness and bias risk assessments.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management activities—as identified in the MAP function—are assessed and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI environmental impact assessment, including evaluating computational resource consumption during model training, assessing sustainability of AI management activities, measuring carbon footprint and energy usage, and documenting environmental impact findings.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
elements.outcome_text: Effectiveness of the employed TEVV metrics and processes are evaluated and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to assess TEVV process effectiveness for AI systems, including evaluating whether testing, evaluation, verification, and validation metrics are achieving their intended purpose, identifying measurement gaps, and documenting process effectiveness findings and recommendations.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
elements.outcome_text: Approaches, personnel, and documentation are in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated, and emergent AI risks based on factors such as intended and actual performance in deployed contexts.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in establishing AI risk tracking infrastructure, including designing approaches for identifying existing, unanticipated, and emergent risks, assigning tracking personnel and responsibilities, and maintaining documentation of risk evolution based on deployed system performance.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
elements.outcome_text: Risk tracking approaches are considered for settings where AI risks are difficult to assess using currently available measurement techniques or where metrics are not yet available.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of risk tracking methodology for unmeasurable or emerging AI risks, including developing proxy indicators for difficult-to-quantify risks, designing qualitative risk tracking approaches, and adapting tracking methods for scenarios where standard measurement techniques are insufficient.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
elements.outcome_text: Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal system outcomes are established and integrated into AI system evaluation metrics.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing end-user and community feedback mechanisms for AI systems, including establishing problem reporting channels, creating outcome appeal processes, and integrating feedback data into AI system evaluation metrics and risk tracking.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Measurement results are communicated.
elements.outcome_text: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended. Results are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI measurement result validation and communication, including soliciting domain expert input on trustworthiness measurements, validating performance consistency across deployment contexts and lifecycle stages, and documenting validated measurement results for stakeholder communication.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: Measurement results are communicated.
elements.outcome_text: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are used to inform the risk management process.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to integrate AI measurement results into risk management decision-making, including translating trustworthiness measurements into risk management actions, updating risk assessments based on measurement findings, and ensuring measurement data flows into GOVERN, MAP, and MANAGE function activities.
elements.function_description: Identified risks are assessed, analyzed, or tracked.

elements.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
elements.outcome_text: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system go/no-go determination, including evaluating whether an AI system achieves its intended purpose against stated objectives, conducting readiness assessments for development progression or deployment, and making evidence-based proceed/halt decisions.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
elements.outcome_text: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritized based on impact, likelihood, and available resources or methods.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI risk prioritization, including ranking risks by impact and likelihood, assessing available mitigation resources and methods, and developing prioritized risk treatment plans that align with organizational capacity and risk tolerance.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
elements.outcome_text: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority, as identified by the MAP and MEASURE functions, are developed, planned, and documented. Risk response options can include mitigating, transferring, avoiding, or accepting.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI risk response strategy development, including designing mitigation measures, evaluating risk transfer options, defining risk avoidance criteria, establishing risk acceptance thresholds, and documenting planned responses for high-priority AI risks identified through MAP and MEASURE activities.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
elements.outcome_text: Negative residual risks (remaining risks) to both downstream acquirers of AI systems and end users are documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI residual risk documentation, including identifying and characterizing negative residual risks after mitigation, documenting residual risks for downstream AI system acquirers and end users, and communicating remaining risk levels to stakeholders.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account—along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches, or methods—to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to conduct AI resource and alternative analysis, including assessing resources needed for AI risk management, evaluating non-AI alternative systems and approaches, and making comparative decisions that reduce potential impact magnitude and likelihood.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Mechanisms are in place and applied to sustain the value of deployed AI systems.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in sustaining deployed AI system value, including implementing mechanisms for ongoing value assessment, maintaining system performance and relevance, and applying continuous improvement practices to ensure deployed AI systems continue delivering intended benefits.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Relevant AI actors are informed of and involved in the management of AI risks.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI risk management stakeholder engagement, including identifying and informing relevant AI actors about risk management activities, involving stakeholders in risk response decisions, and maintaining communication channels for ongoing risk management participation.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
elements.outcome_text: Mechanisms are in place and applied, and approaches are documented, to enable AI actors to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and implementation.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing AI feedback incorporation systems, including establishing adjudication mechanisms for stakeholder feedback, documenting feedback integration approaches, and ensuring regular incorporation of validated feedback into AI system design and implementation processes.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.
elements.outcome_text: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored, and risk controls are applied and documented.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in third-party AI risk monitoring and control, including implementing regular monitoring of AI risks from third-party resources, applying and documenting risk controls for third-party AI components, and maintaining ongoing assessment of third-party AI benefit realization.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.
elements.outcome_text: Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored as part of AI system regular monitoring and maintenance.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of pre-trained model monitoring requirements, including integrating pre-trained model monitoring into AI system maintenance workflows, tracking model drift and performance degradation, and assessing pre-trained model risks as part of regular AI system monitoring activities.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
elements.outcome_text: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors, appeal and override, decommissioning, incident response, recovery, and change management.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in post-deployment AI system monitoring and incident management, including implementing user feedback capture mechanisms, establishing appeal and override procedures, planning decommissioning protocols, designing incident response and recovery processes, and managing AI system changes throughout the operational lifecycle.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.

elements.category_title: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
elements.outcome_text: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI continuous improvement methodology, including integrating measurable improvement activities into AI system update cycles, establishing regular engagement cadences with interested parties and AI actors, and tracking improvement metrics across AI system iterations.
elements.function_description: AI risks are managed.
